name: Claude Sonnet 5
description: Anthropic's flagship model, tuned for careful reasoning, coding, and long agentic tool-use sessions.
strengths: Strong, reliable coding and debugging across large codebases
Careful, well-hedged reasoning on ambiguous questions
Long agentic sessions (multi-step tool use) without losing the thread
weaknesses: Pricier than budget-tier models for high-volume API use
More conservative/hedged tone than some competitors prefer
Smaller context window than Gemini's largest tier

name: GPT-5.1
description: OpenAI's unified flagship model, blending fast responses with an adjustable deep-reasoning mode and native multimodality.
strengths: Broad all-around competence across writing, coding, and analysis
Very large installed base of integrations and tooling
weaknesses: Reasoning-mode responses can be noticeably slower
Context window trails Gemini's largest tier

name: Gemini 3 Pro
description: Google's flagship model, built around a very large context window and native video/audio/image understanding.
strengths: Largest practical context window of the major models — whole codebases or books at once
Best-in-class native video and audio understanding
Tight integration with Google Workspace and Search
weaknesses: Very long contexts can dilute attention to early content
Creative writing voice seen as slightly more generic by some users
Pricing steps up above the 200K-token tier

name: Grok 4
description: xAI's flagship model, with real-time access to X/Twitter data and a faster, more permissive response style.
strengths: Real-time awareness of current events and social conversation
Less filtered/more direct answers as a stylistic choice
weaknesses: Accuracy more variable on niche factual questions
Less proven track record in enterprise/business settings

name: DeepSeek V3.2
description: An aggressively priced, open-weight-friendly model line with strong math and coding benchmarks relative to cost.
strengths: Exceptional cost-to-performance ratio, especially for coding and math
weaknesses: Weaker creative writing voice than closed frontier models
No polished consumer subscription tier or app ecosystem
Smaller context window than the other flagships here

name: Llama 4 Maverick
description: Meta's open-weight flagship model, designed to be self-hosted, fine-tuned, and run cheaply at scale via third-party providers.
strengths: Open weights — self-hostable and fine-tunable for custom use cases
No first-party per-token pricing lock-in; competitive third-party hosting
Free to use via the Meta AI consumer app
weaknesses: Trails the closed frontier labs on the hardest reasoning and coding benchmarks
Quality varies by which third-party host/provider you use
No official first-party consumer subscription with premium features
